findings.title: Repair broken checklist cross-references in spec risk sections
findings.evidence: spec.md cites `CHK-008`, `CHK-017`, and `CHK-005`, but checklist.md has no such rows; the matching verification rows are CHK-034, CHK-041, and CHK-023 instead.
findings.impact: Packet-local mitigation paths point reviewers to nonexistent verification items, breaking traceability inside the canonical docs and making risk controls unverifiable.
findings.recommendation: Replace the stale CHK references in spec.md with the actual checklist row IDs that cover grep closure, sk-doc validation, and resume round-trip testing.

findings.title: Align closeout status with unmet validation and manual test gates
findings.evidence: implementation-summary.md marks `validate.sh --strict` as `CONDITIONAL — 1 error, 3 warnings` and defers manual tests T039-T043, while spec.md REQ-011/REQ-017 and checklist.md CHK-022/023/040/041 require those gates to pass.
findings.impact: The packet reads as nearly complete even though multiple P0 acceptance gates remain unresolved, which can mislead downstream reviewers and memory consumers.
findings.recommendation: Either resolve the outstanding validation/manual-test gates or downgrade the implementation summary's completion narrative and next-step guidance so it no longer implies closeout readiness.

findings.title: Eliminate duplicated intake questionnaire blocks from merged command surfaces
findings.evidence: The packet claims `/spec_kit:plan` and `/spec_kit:complete` now reference `intake-contract.md` instead of carrying inline intake blocks, but both command files still inline the full Intake Q0-Q4+ question set almost verbatim.
findings.impact: The supposed single source of truth is still duplicated across the live command prompts, so future intake-contract edits can drift from plan/complete again and the packet's zero-duplication acceptance criteria remain unmet.
findings.recommendation: Replace the duplicated question blocks in both command files with a concise pointer to `intake-contract.md §5` or generate the shared block from one source.

findings.title: Normalize seven-step versus eight-step plan workflow terminology
findings.evidence: The live command header still describes `/spec_kit:plan` as a `Planning workflow (7 steps)`, while the packet plan and implementation summary both describe the merged workflow as `8-step` with intake as Step 1.
findings.impact: Operators reading different surfaces get conflicting mental models for the same command, which makes downstream docs and reviews harder to trust.
findings.recommendation: Pick one authoritative counting model and update either the command header or the packet docs so they describe the same workflow structure.

findings.title: Implement a real intake-only stop path in plan YAML workflows
findings.evidence: plan.md documents `--intake-only` as halting after Emit and skipping planning Steps 1-7, but neither `spec_kit_plan_auto.yaml` nor `spec_kit_plan_confirm.yaml` contains any `intake_only` branch; both workflows bind inline intake data in step `0.5` and then continue directly into `step_1_request_analysis`.
findings.impact: The advertised `/spec_kit:plan --intake-only` runtime behavior is not actually wired in the executable YAML workflows, so the flag can fall through into normal planning instead of stopping after trio publication.
findings.recommendation: Add an explicit `intake_only` gate to both plan YAMLs that terminates successfully after Emit and bypasses Steps 1-7.

findings.title: Rename leftover start-delegation workflow labels after deleting /spec_kit:start
findings.evidence: The live plan YAMLs still use `step_0.5_start_delegation_branch` and emit `start_delegation_triggered` / `start_delegation_completed` events even though the separate `/spec_kit:start` command was deleted.
findings.impact: Telemetry and operator debugging surfaces keep referring to a deleted command surface, which obscures whether failures belong to the new shared intake contract or the removed command.
findings.recommendation: Rename the branch and event identifiers to intake-contract terminology so workflow traces match the shipped architecture.

findings.title: Remove deleted start command from forward-looking command indexes
findings.evidence: `.opencode/command/README.txt` still describes the `spec_kit` namespace as containing `start`, and `.opencode/command/spec_kit/README.txt` still has an agent-delegation row for `start`, even though `/spec_kit:start` and its assets were deleted.
findings.impact: The forward-looking command indexes still advertise a deleted command surface, so operators following the docs can be routed to a command that no longer exists.
findings.recommendation: Delete the stale `start` entries from both command README files and replace any needed guidance with `/spec_kit:plan --intake-only`.

findings.title: Remove nonexistent agent paths from system-spec-kit graph metadata
findings.evidence: graph-metadata.json still lists `.opencode/agent/speckit.md` and `.claude/agents/speckit.md` in `derived.key_files`, but neither file exists and `skill_graph_compiler.py --validate-only` fails on both missing paths.
findings.impact: The skill-graph validation harness exits non-zero for the system-spec-kit surface, so graph-health checks are broken even though normal routing still loads.
findings.recommendation: Replace the stale `speckit.md` references with the actual runtime agent files or remove the dead entries from `derived.key_files`.

findings.title: Replace manual template-copy quick starts with the canonical intake workflow
findings.evidence: The templates root README now points operators to `/spec_kit:plan --intake-only` as the standalone intake entry, but the level README quick-start blocks still instruct users to create `specs/...` folders manually and copy `*.md` files directly.
findings.impact: Operators following the README examples can bypass canonical trio/bootstrap behavior such as metadata generation, folder-state classification, and repair handling, leaving the template docs out of sync with the shipped lifecycle.
findings.recommendation: Rewrite the quick-start sections to use `/spec_kit:plan --intake-only`, or explicitly mark raw template copying as maintainer-only guidance.

findings.title: Align Level 3+ workflow notes with lower-level continuity guidance
findings.evidence: Level 2 and Level 3 template READMEs document `/spec_kit:resume` and `/memory:save` continuity behavior, but Level 3+ workflow notes only cover approvals, checklist evidence, and finalizing `implementation-summary.md`.
findings.impact: The template documentation set becomes asymmetric at the highest governance level, which makes lifecycle guidance harder to follow consistently.
findings.recommendation: Add the same resume/save continuity notes to the Level 3+ README that appear in the Level 2 and Level 3 workflow notes.

findings.title: Rename leftover start-delegation identifiers in complete YAML workflows
findings.evidence: The complete YAMLs still use `start_delegation_contract`, `step_0.5_start_delegation_branch`, and `start_delegation_triggered` / `start_delegation_completed` events even though the shared intake contract replaced the separate `/spec_kit:start` surface.
findings.impact: Telemetry and operator inspection of the completion workflows still expose removed command vocabulary, leaving the rename only partially finished.
findings.recommendation: Rename the complete-workflow contract, step, and event identifiers to intake-contract terminology so all YAML assets reflect the shipped architecture consistently.

findings.title: Add the spec-packet governance route to Copilot delegation docs
findings.evidence: Claude, Codex, and Gemini delegation references each include a spec-packet routing lane that points conductors back to `Main agent + /spec_kit:plan --intake-only`, but the Copilot routing guide only lists generic explore/task/cloud choices with no SpecKit packet route.
findings.impact: Copilot conductors do not receive the same distributed-governance guardrail as the other runtimes, making it easier to route packet-document work directly to a delegated executor instead of the canonical intake flow.
findings.recommendation: Add a spec-packet routing row or quick-selection line to the Copilot delegation reference that mirrors the other runtimes and points packet creation/repair back through `/spec_kit:plan --intake-only` (fallback `/spec_kit:plan`) plus `/spec_kit:resume` for continuity.
